items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения» and keeps the parallel structure that sets up the comedic suspicion callback.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; keeps the repetitive Soviet-formula politeness and the era-clash humor of Bunsha’s drunken self-introduction.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; preserves the abrupt authoritarian performative and comic escalation.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; maintains the elevated/archaic melancholy tone that contrasts with the mundane Soviet setting and keeps the insult-comedy of «старушка».

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; keeps demons as the agent and preserves the comic misinterpretation of modern technology as sorcery.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; retains the mock-biblical proclamation and the atheist-society irony.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; conveys the MC-style command that triggers mass dancing and matches the comedic release of decorum.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; keeps the breezy self-satisfied criminal tone and the ‘lucky find’ Soviet-deficit resonance.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preserving the joke’s clarity for non-Russian viewers while keeping the satirical inversion.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; preserves the comic inflation-by-enumeration and the self-betraying counting rhythm.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference translation; keeps the calm, sing-song fatalism that turns the psychiatric threat into shared absurdist comedy.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
